source: President Ibrahim Mohamed Solih inaugurated the new hospital facility on the island.
correct: ރައީސުލްޖުމްހޫރިއްޔާ އިބްރާހީމް މުޙައްމަދު ޞާލިހު ރަށުގައި ހެދި އާ ހޮސްޕިޓަލް ހުޅުއްވައިދެއްވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ހިޒް އެކްސެލެންސީ އިބްރާހީމް މުޙައްމަދު ޞާލިހު ރަށުގައި ހެދި އާ ހޮސްޕިޓަލް ހުޅުއްވައިދެއްވިއެވެ.
error_description: PO convention: Maldivian president is referred to as ރައީސުލްޖުމްހޫރިއްޔާ (full title), never as ހިޒް އެކްސެލެންސީ (H.E. transliteration). H.E. is used only for foreign heads of state in Dhivehi PO text.

source: His Excellency the President of Turkey congratulated President Solih on the occasion.
correct: ތުރުކީގެ ރައީސް ރެދްޖެޕް ތައްޔިޕް އެރްދޯޣަން، ރައީސް ޞާލިހަށް ތަހުނިޔާ ފޮނުއްވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ހިޒް އެކްސެލެންސީ ތުރުކީ ވިލާތުގެ ރައީސް، ރައީސް ޞާލިހަށް ތަހުނިޔާ ފޮނުއްވިއެވެ.
error_description: PO convention for foreign heads of state: name + country title, no H.E. transliteration in Dhivehi prose.

source: The Minister of Finance attended the budget session.
correct: ދައުލަތުގެ ވަޒީރުލްމާލިއްޔަތު ބަޖެޓް ޖަލްސާގައި ބައިވެރިވެ ވަޑައިގެންނެވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ފިނޭންސް މިނިސްޓަރ ބަޖެޓް ޖަލްސާގައި ބައިވެރިވެ ވަޑައިގެންނެވިއެވެ.
error_description: Formal PO register: ވަޒީރުލްމާލިއްޔަތު (Arabic-derived Finance Minister title) vs ފިނޭންސް މިނިސްޓަރ (English transliteration). PO press releases use the formal Arabic-derived form.

source: The Vice President chaired the committee meeting.
correct: ނައިބު ރައީސް ކޮމިޓީ ބައްދަލުުވުމުގެ ރިޔާސަތު ބެލެހެއްޓެވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ވައިސް ޕްރެޒިޑެންޓް ކޮމިޓީ ބައްދަލުުވުމުގެ ރިޔާސަތު ބެލެހެއްޓެވިއެވެ.
error_description: ނައިބު ރައީސް is the correct Dhivehi title for Vice President; ވައިސް ޕްރެޒިޑެންޓް is a transliteration error not used in PO text.

source: The Attorney General presented the legal opinion to the Cabinet.
correct: ދައުލަތުގެ ބަންޑާރަ ނައިބު ކެބިނެޓަށް ޤާނޫނީ ލަފާ ހުށަހަޅުއްވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ދި އެޓޯރނީ ޖެނެރަލް ކެބިނެޓަށް ލީގަލް ލަފާ ހުށަހަޅުއްވިއެވެ.
error_description: ދައުލަތުގެ ބަންޑާރަ ނައިބު is the correct PO form for Attorney General; transliteration is not used in formal DV text.

source: The delegation visited the islands of Haa Alif Atoll during the tour.
correct: ވަފުދު ދަތުރުގައި ހއ. އަތޮޅުގެ ރަށްތަކަށް ޒިޔާރަތްކުރިއެވެ.
incorrect: ވަފުދު ދަތުރުގައި ހައި ހުރި ޢަލީ ދިވެހި ރަށްތަކަށް ޒިޔާރަތްކުރިއެވެ.
error_description: ހއ. (two Thaana letters + period) is the standard PO abbreviation for Haa Alif Atoll. Phonetic approximations of the English name are wrong.

source: The President inaugurated a new health centre in Thaa Atoll.
correct: ރައީސް ތ. އަތޮޅުގެ ސިއްހީ މަރުކަޒު ހުޅުއްވައިދެއްވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ރައީސް ތާ ހުރި އަތޮޅުުގެ ސިއްހީ މަރުކަޒު ހުޅުއްވައިދެއްވިއެވެ.
error_description: ތ. is the standard PO abbreviation for Thaa Atoll. Full romanised expansion or phonetic approximation is an error.

source: The ceremony was held at Addu City.
correct: ރަސްމިއްޔާތު ބޭއްވީ އައްޑޫ ސިޓީގައެވެ.
incorrect: ރަސްމިއްޔާތު ބޭއްވީ އެޑޫ ސިޓީ ގައެވެ.
error_description: އައްޑޫ is the correct Thaana spelling of Addu. Phonetic anglicisation (އެޑޫ) is not used in PO text. Also: no space before ގައި (postposition attaches directly).

source: The Ministry of Islamic Affairs issued the announcement.
correct: ދީނީ ކަންތައްތަކާ ބެހޭ ވުޒާރާ އިން ފޮނުވި ޢިލާނުގައި ވަނީ.
incorrect: ދި މިނިސްޓްރީ އޮފް އިސްލާމިކް އެފެއާޒް ޢިލާނު ނެރެފިއެވެ.
error_description: ދީނީ ކަންތައްތަކާ ބެހޭ ވުޒާރާ is the official Dhivehi name for the Ministry of Islamic Affairs; English transliteration is wrong in formal DV.

source: The Maldivian Democratic Party submitted the petition.
correct: މޯލްޑިވިއަން ޑިމޮކްރެޓިކް ޕާޓީ (އެމްޑީޕީ) އިން ޕެޓިޝަން ހުށަހެޅިއެވެ.
incorrect: ދިވެހި ޑިމޮކްރެޓިކް ޕާޓީ ޕެޓިޝަން ހުށަހެޅިއެވެ.
error_description: The official Dhivehi rendering of MDP uses the transliteration މޯލްޑިވިއަން ޑިމޮކްރެޓިކް ޕާޓީ (not a back-translation as ދިވެހި ޑިމޮކްރެޓިކް ޕާޓީ). Party names use PO-registered transliterations.

source: The meeting was scheduled for 15 March 2024.
correct: ބައްދަލުުވުން ތާވަލުކުރީ 15 މާރިޗު 2024 ވަނަ ދުވަހަށެވެ.
incorrect: ބައްދަލުުވުން ތާވަލުކުރީ ١٥ مارس ٢٠٢٤ ވަނަ ދުވަހަށެވެ.
error_description: PO convention: Western Arabic numerals (15, 2024) and Dhivehi month names (މާރިޗު for March), NOT Arabic-Indic numerals or Arabic month names.

source: The total budget allocation is USD 2.5 million.
correct: ޖުމްލަ ބަޖެޓްގެ ޢަދަދަކީ 2.5 މިލިއަން ޑޮލަރެވެ.
incorrect: ޖުމްލަ ބަޖެޓްގެ ޢަދަދަކީ ٢٫٥ ملیون ڈالر ہے.
error_description: PO convention: Western Arabic numerals for quantities; ޑޮލަރ (Thaana transliteration) not Arabic script. Mixing Arabic script with Thaana is a script fidelity error.

source: The ceremony was held on the occasion of the 56th Independence Day.
correct: ރަސްމިއްޔާތު ބޭއްވީ 56 ވަނަ މިނިވަން ދުވަހުގެ މުނާސަބަތުގައެވެ.
incorrect: ރަސްމިއްޔާތު ބޭއްވީ ފަންސާސް ހަ ވަނަ މިނިވަން ދުވަހުގެ މުނާސަބަތުގައެވެ.
error_description: PO convention: numerals in figures (56 ވަނަ), not spelled out in words. Spelled-out numerals (ފަންސާސް ހަ) are informal and not used in PO press releases.

source: ރައީސުލްޖުމްހޫރިއްޔާ ބޭރު ދަތުރުފުޅެއްގައި ފުރާވަޑައިގެންނެވިއެވެ.
correct: The President departed on an overseas trip.
incorrect: رئیس الجمهوریة غادر في رحلة خارجية.
error_description: Cat-7 script fidelity: frontier LLMs sometimes produce Arabic script output for Dhivehi input. Any Arabic codepoint (U+0600–U+06FF) in the output of a DV→EN task is a hard failure.

source: The Maldives achieved independence on 26 July 1965.
correct: ދިވެހިރާއްޖެ 26 ޖުލައި 1965 ގައި މިނިވަން ވިއެވެ.
incorrect: Dhivehi raajje 26 Julai 1965 gai minevan vieve.
error_description: Cat-7 script fidelity: romanised Dhivehi output (Dhivehin) instead of Thaana script. Any EN→DV output without Thaana codepoints (U+0780–U+07BF) is a hard failure.

source: The minister said she would attend the conference.
correct: ވަޒީރު ވިދާޅުވީ ކޮންފަރެންސްގައި ބައިވެރިވެ ވަޑައިގަންނަވާނެ ކަމަށެވެ.
incorrect: ވަޒީރު ވިދާޅުވީ ކޮންފަރެންސްގައި ބައިވެރިވެ ވަޑައިގަންނަވާނެ ކަމަށެވެ. (ހިނގި)
error_description: Dhivehi does not grammatically mark gender on third-person pronouns or verbs. EN she/he → DV: the translation drops the gender marking; a calqued feminine marker does not exist in Dhivehi.
notes: Needs native DV speaker review. The correct translation is identical for he/she — the test is that the model does NOT introduce a spurious gender marking.

source: ޑޮކްޓަރ ވިދާޅުވީ ދުވަސްވީ ބަލި ކަލޭފާނު ގެ ހާލު ރަނގަޅު ކަމަށެވެ.
correct: The doctor said the elderly patient's condition was good.
incorrect: The doctor said the elderly female patient's condition was good.
error_description: DV→EN direction: Dhivehi gender-neutral pronoun/reference must not be rendered with a gendered EN pronoun unless context disambiguates. Adding 'female' with no DV basis is an error.
notes: Needs native DV speaker review for the source sentence accuracy.

source: The state budget for the upcoming fiscal year was presented to the parliament.
correct: ކުރިއަށް އޮތް އަހަރަށް ލަފާކުރެވޭ ދައުލަތުގެ ބަޖެޓު ރައްޔިތުންގެ މަޖިލީހަށް ހުށަހަޅައިފިއެވެ.
incorrect: ދެން އޮތް ފިސްކަލް ޔިއަރ ގެ ސްޓޭޓް ބަޖެޓް ޕާލިމެންޓަށް ޕްރެޒެންޓް ކޮށްފިއެވެ.
error_description: ދައުލަތުގެ ބަޖެޓު is the established PO term for 'state budget'; ބަޖެޓު attaches without space. ރައްޔިތުންގެ މަޖިލީހަށް is correct for 'parliament'. Transliterated English terms (ފިސްކަލް ޔިއަރ, ޕާލިމެންޓް) are wrong in formal PO context.

source: The Cabinet convened an extraordinary session.
correct: ކެބިނެޓްގެ ހާއްސަ ޖަލްސާ ބޭއްވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ކެބިނެޓ ހިންގި ސްޕެޝަލް ސެޝަން ވަރަށް ކުރުކޮށެވެ.
error_description: ކެބިނެޓް (with final ތ marker) is the PO form. ހާއްސަ ޖަލްސާ is established for 'extraordinary session'; ސްޕެޝަލް ސެޝަން is a transliteration error.

source: The Supreme Court issued its ruling on the constitutional matter.
correct: ސުޕްރީމް ކޯޓުން ޤާނޫނުއަސާސީގެ މައްސަލައިގައި ހުކުމް ކޮށްފިއެވެ.
incorrect: ހަޔަރ ކޯޓުން ކޮންސްޓިޓިއުޝަނަލް ކޭސް ގަ ވަރްޑިކްޓް ނެރެފިއެވެ.
error_description: ސުޕްރީމް ކޯޓު is the PO-established term for Supreme Court. ޤާނޫނުއަސާސީ is the correct term for 'constitutional'; ހުކުމް for 'ruling'. Free substitution of English transliterations is wrong.

source: The President stated that the economy has improved significantly.
correct: ރައީސް ވިދާޅުވިއެވެ: އިޤްތިޞާދަށް ބޮޑު ތަރައްޤީ ލިބިފައިވާ ކަމަށެވެ.
incorrect: ރައީސް ބުންޏެވެ: އިޤްތިޞާދު ރަނގަޅުވެއްޖެ.
error_description: Register mismatch: formal PO uses ވިދާޅުވިއެވެ (honorific speech verb + -ވިއެވެ formal suffix) for presidential speech. ބުންޏެވެ is standard (non-honorific) and -ވިއެވެ must follow the clause.
notes: Needs native DV speaker review for morphological accuracy of both variants.

source: The official said the project will be completed next year.
correct: ރަސްމީ ބޭފުޅާ ވިދާޅުވިއެވެ، ޕްރޮޖެކްޓް ނިމޭނީ ކުރިއަށް އޮތް އަހަރު ކަމަށެވެ.
incorrect: ރަސްމީ ބޭފުޅާ ބުނި، ޕްރޮޖެކްޓް ނިމޭނީ ކުރިއަށް އޮތް އަހަރޭ.
error_description: Register mismatch: formal institutional text requires ވިދާޅުވިއެވެ and reported speech ending in ކަމަށެވެ. Informal forms (-ނީ ... އޭ construction) are colloquial and wrong in PO register.

source: I will attend the ceremony on behalf of the government.
correct: ތިމަންނަ، ސަރުކާރުގެ ފަރާތުން ރަސްމިއްޔާތަށް ހާޒިރުވާ ހުށީމެވެ.
incorrect: އަހަރެން ސަރުކާރުގެ ފަރާތުން ރަސްމިއްޔާތަށް ހާޒިރުވާ ހުށީމެވެ.
error_description: Register mismatch: first-person pronoun. ތިމަންނަ (or ތިމަންނާ) is the formal/humble first-person for use in official/ceremonial contexts. އަހަރެން is informal/colloquial.
notes: Needs native DV speaker verification. The pronoun choice is linguistically attested but the full sentence needs checking.

source: Justice Aisha Mohamed delivered the Supreme Court judgment.
correct: ފަނޑިޔާރު ޢާއިޝާ މުޙައްމަދު ސުޕްރީމް ކޯޓްގެ ހުކުމް ކިޔައިދެއްވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ރައީސުލްޖުމްހޫރިއްޔާ ޢާއިޝާ މުޙައްމަދު ސުޕްރީމް ކޯޓްގެ ހުކުމް ކިޔައިދެއްވިއެވެ.
error_description: ފަނޑިޔާރު is the correct honorific title for a justice or judge. Using ރައީސުލްޖުމްހޫރިއްޔާ (the presidential title) for a justice is a serious category error that confuses the head of state with the judiciary.

source: The Speaker of Parliament opened the sitting.
correct: ރައްޔިތުންގެ މަޖިލީހުގެ ރައީސް ޖަލްސާ ފައްޓަވައިދެއްވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ދަ ސްޕީކަރ އޮފް ޕާލަމެންޓް ޖަލްސާ ފައްޓަވައިދެއްވިއެވެ.
error_description: ރައްޔިތުންގެ މަޖިލީހުގެ ރައީސް is the correct Dhivehi title for the Speaker of Parliament. Direct calque-translation from English (ދަ ސްޕީކަރ އޮފް ޕާލަމެންޓް) is a transliteration error not used in formal PO text.

source: The Ambassador of Japan presented his credentials to the President.
correct: ޖަޕާނުގެ ސަފީރު ރައީސުލްޖުމްހޫރިއްޔާއަށް ސިފަތު ސިޓީ ފޮނުުވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ޖަޕާނުގެ އެމްބާ-ސަ-ޑަރ ރައީސުލްޖުމްހޫރިއްޔާއަށް ސިފަތު ސިޓީ ފޮނުުވިއެވެ.
error_description: ސަފީރު is the established Dhivehi term for Ambassador (from Arabic سفير). Transliteration variants of the English word 'Ambassador' (e.g. އެމްބާ-ސަ-ޑަރ or އެންބެސެޑަރ) are not used in PO Dhivehi.

source: The Governor of the Maldives Monetary Authority addressed the press conference.
correct: މޯލްޑިވްސް މަނިޓަރީ އޮތޯރިޓީގެ ގަވަރުނަރ ނޫސްވެރިންގެ ބައްދަލުުވުމުގައި ވާހަކަ ދެއްކެވިއެވެ.
incorrect: މޯލްޑިވްސް މަނިޓަރީ އޮތޯރިޓީގެ ގަވަރުނަރ-ޖެނެރަލް-ޗީފް-އެގްޒެކިއުޓިވް-ހެޑް ނޫސްވެރިންގެ ބައްދަލުުވުމުގައި ވާހަކަ ދެއްކެވިއެވެ.
error_description: ގަވަރުނަރ is the correct and accepted PO transliteration for the MMA Governor title. Elongated English-derived compound forms (ގަވަރުނަރ-ޖެނެރަލް-ޗީފް etc.) do not exist in formal Dhivehi institutional usage.

source: The Presidential Advisor on Climate Change issued a statement.
correct: ރައީސްގެ ލަފާދޭ ބޭފުުޅެ ކްލައިމެޓް ޗޭންޖާ ގުޅޭ ބަޔާން ނެރުުއްވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ދަ ޕްރެޒިޑެންޝަލް ޕޮލިސީ އެޑްވައިޒަރ ކްލައިމެޓް ޗޭންޖާ ގުޅޭ ސްޓޭޓްމެންޓް ދޫކޮށްލިއެވެ.
error_description: ރައީސްގެ ލަފާދޭ ބޭފުުޅެ is the correct Dhivehi construction for a presidential advisor. Calquing the English title directly as ޕްރެޒިޑެންޝަލް ޕޮލިސީ އެޑްވައިޒަރ is a transliteration error; ސްޓޭޓްމެންޓް should be ބަޔާން.

source: The project was launched in Baa Atoll.
correct: ޕްރޮޖެކްޓް ފެށީ ބ. އަތޮޅުގައެވެ.
incorrect: ޕްރޮޖެކްޓް ފެށީ ބާ-ހުރި-އަތޮޅު ގައެވެ.
error_description: ބ. (single Thaana letter + period) is the standard PO abbreviation for Baa Atoll. Any phonetic approximation or expansion of the English name is an error in formal PO Dhivehi text.

source: A new school was opened in Raa Atoll.
correct: ރ. އަތޮޅުގައި އާ ސްކޫލެއް ހުޅުވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ރާ-އަތޮޅު ގައި އާ ސްކޫލެއް ހުޅުވިއެވެ.
error_description: ރ. (single Thaana letter + period) is the standard PO abbreviation for Raa Atoll. Hyphenated expansions or phonetic forms are not used in formal PO text.

source: The health team visited Noonu Atoll this week.
correct: ސިއްހީ ޓީމު މި ހަފްތާ ނ. އަތޮޅަށް ޒިޔާރަތްކުރިއެވެ.
incorrect: ސިއްހީ ޓީމު މި ހަފްތާ ނޫނު-އަތޮޅަށް ޒިޔާރަތްކުރިއެވެ.
error_description: ނ. (single Thaana letter + period) is the standard PO abbreviation for Noonu Atoll. The expanded form ނޫނު-އަތޮޅު is not the correct PO convention; the abbreviation format must be used.

source: The event took place in Male', the capital city.
correct: ރަސްމިއްޔާތު ބޭއްވީ ވެރިރަށް މާލޭގައެވެ.
incorrect: ރަސްމިއްޔާތު ބޭއްވީ ވެރިރަށް މާލޭ ގައެވެ.
error_description: މާލެ is the correct Thaana spelling for Male' (the capital). The postposition -ގައި attaches directly without a space: މާލޭގައި. Adding a space before the postposition is a grammatical spacing error common in model output.

source: The new city of Hulhumale hosted the international conference.
correct: ދިވެހިރާއްޖޭގެ ހުޅުމާލޭގައި ބައިނަލްއަޤްވާމީ ކޮންފަރެންސް ބޭއްވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ދިވެހިރާއްޖޭގެ ހުލޫ-މާ-ލޭ ގައި ބައިނަލްއަޤްވާމީ ކޮންފަރެންސް ބޭއްވިއެވެ.
error_description: ހުޅުމާލެ is the correct Thaana spelling of Hulhumale. Hyphenated or fragmented phonetic renderings (ހުލޫ-މާ-ލޭ) are wrong; postposition -ގައި attaches without a space.

source: Tourism revenue increased by 30 percent this year.
correct: މި އަހަރު ފަތުރުވެރިކަމުގެ ޢާމްދަނީ 30 އިންސައްތަ ވަނީ އިތުުރުވެފައެވެ.
incorrect: މި އަހަރު ފަތުރުވެރިކަމުގެ ޢާމްދަނީ ٣٠٪ ވަނީ އިތުުރުވެފައެވެ.
error_description: PO convention: percentage expressed as Western Arabic numeral + Dhivehi word (30 އިންސައްތަ). Using Arabic-Indic numerals with the Arabic percent sign (٣٠٪) is a script fidelity error.

source: The government allocated MVR 500 million for the project.
correct: ސަރުކާރުން ޕްރޮޖެކްޓަށް 500 މިލިއަން ރުފިޔާ ޚަރަދުކުރިއެވެ.
incorrect: ސަރުކާރުން ޕްރޮޖެކްޓަށް ފަސްސަތޭކަ މިލިއަން ރުުފިޔާ ޚަރަދުކުރިއެވެ.
error_description: PO convention for large currency amounts: Western Arabic numerals (500) followed by the unit word (މިލިއަން ރުފިޔާ). Spelling out the numeral in Dhivehi words (ފަސްސަތޭކަ) is not the PO style for figures in press releases.

source: The project was completed in the year 2024.
correct: ޕްރޮޖެކްޓް ނިމުނީ 2024 ވަނަ އަހަރުއެވެ.
incorrect: ޕްރޮޖެކްޓް ނިމުނީ ދެ ހާސް ވިހި ހަތަރު ވަނަ އަހަރުއެވެ.
error_description: PO convention: years are always written in Western Arabic numerals (2024 ވަނަ އަހަރު). Spelling out the year in Dhivehi words (ދެ ހާސް ވިހި ހަތަރު) is informal and not used in PO institutional text.

source: The matter is governed by Article 94 of the Constitution.
correct: މި ކަން ބިނާވަނީ ޤާނޫނުއަސާސީގެ 94 ވަނަ މާއްދާ ގެ މައްޗަށެވެ.
incorrect: މި ކަން ބިނާވަނީ ޤާނޫނުއަސާސީގެ ނުވަ ދިހަ ހަތަ ވަނަ މާއްދާ ގެ މައްޗަށެވެ.
error_description: Article and clause numbers in legal texts follow PO convention: Western Arabic numeral + ވަނަ + މާއްދާ (e.g. 94 ވަނަ މާއްދާ). Spelling out the number in words is incorrect and also factually wrong here (ނުވަ ދިހަ ހަތަ = 97, not 94).

source: The ceremony will begin at 10:00 AM.
correct: ރަސްމިއްޔާތު ފެށޭނީ ހެނދުުނު 10:00 ގައެވެ.
incorrect: ރަސްމިއްޔާތު ފެށޭނީ ١٠:٠٠ ص ގައެވެ.
error_description: PO time convention: Dhivehi time-of-day word (ހެނދުުނު for morning/AM) + Western Arabic clock numerals (10:00). Using Arabic-Indic clock numerals with the Arabic AM marker (١٠:٠٠ ص) is a script fidelity error.

source: The President will visit the northern atolls next month.
correct: ރައީސް ކުރިއަށް އޮތް މަހު އުތުރުގެ އަތޮޅުތަކަށް ވަޑައިގަންނަވާނެއެވެ.
incorrect: Raees kuriyas oiy mahu uthuru atholhuthakah vadhai gannavannaeve.
error_description: Cat-7 hard failure: EN→DV task produced romanised Dhivehi (Latin script) instead of Thaana. Any output for a DV target that contains only Latin codepoints and lacks Thaana codepoints (U+0780–U+07BF) is a hard script fidelity failure.

source: ދިވެހި ސަރުކާރުން ރައްޔިތުންނަށް ތައުލީމީ ފުުރުސަތު ފަހި ކޮށްދިނެވެ.
correct: The Maldivian government provided educational opportunities to its citizens.
incorrect: The ދިވެހި ސަރުކާރު provided ތައުލީމީ ފުުރުސަތު to the ރައްޔިތުން.
error_description: Cat-7 hard failure: DV→EN task produced an output mixing Thaana codepoints into the English sentence. The target language is English; any Thaana codepoints (U+0780–U+07BF) in EN-target output constitute a script leakage failure.

source: ރާއްޖޭގެ ދީނީ ކަންތައްތަކާ ބެހޭ ވުޒާރާ ހުކުރު ދުވަހު ބަޔާން ނެރެފިއެވެ.
correct: The Ministry of Islamic Affairs issued a statement on Friday.
incorrect: الوزارة alhukuraa dhuvas issued a بیان on جمعة.
error_description: Cat-7 hard failure: DV→EN task produced output mixing Arabic script (U+0600–U+06FF) with Latin and Thaana. The target is English; Arabic codepoints and Thaana codepoints in the output constitute a compound script contamination failure.

source: The bill was passed by Parliament last night.
correct: ބިލު ރޭ ރައްޔިތުންގެ މަޖިލީހުން ފާސްކޮށްފިއެވެ.
incorrect: ބިލު ރޭ ޕާލިމެންޓުން ފާސްކޮށްފިއެވެ.
error_description: ރައްޔިތުންގެ މަޖިލިސް is the official Dhivehi name for the Maldivian Parliament. Using the transliteration ޕާލިމެންޓް in place of the official name is an error in formal PO text; the proper name must be used.

source: The amendment is consistent with the Constitution of the Maldives.
correct: އިސްލާހު ދިވެހިރާއްޖޭގެ ޤާނޫނުއަސާސީއާ އެއްގޮތްވެއެވެ.
incorrect: އިސްލާހު ދިވެހިރާއްޖޭގެ ކޮންސްޓިޓިއުޝަނާ އެއްގޮތްވެއެވެ.
error_description: ޤާނޫނުއަސާސީ is the correct and established Dhivehi term for the Constitution (from Arabic قانون أساسي). The transliteration ކޮންސްޓިޓިއުޝަން is never used in formal PO text when referring to the Maldivian Constitution.

source: The meeting was held at the Presidential Palace.
correct: ބައްދަލުުވުން ބޭއްވީ ރިޔާސީ ގަނޑުވަރުގައެވެ.
incorrect: ބައްދަލުުވުން ބޭއްވީ ރައީސް ގޭގައެވެ.
error_description: ރިޔާސީ ގަނޑުވަރު is the correct formal term for the Presidential Palace. The colloquial phrase ރައީސް ގޭ (literally 'President's house') is informal and inappropriate for PO press releases.

source: The state will bear the cost of the programme.
correct: ދައުުލަތުން ޕްރޮގްރާމްގެ ޚަރަދު ހިންގާނެއެވެ.
incorrect: ސްޓޭޓުން ޕްރޮގްރާމްގެ ޚަރަދު ހިންގާނެއެވެ.
error_description: ދައުުލަތ is the correct full Dhivehi form for 'state' (from Arabic دولة) and is used in all formal PO text. The transliteration ސްޓޭޓ is not used in institutional Dhivehi writing.

source: The new policy will benefit the general public.
correct: އާ ސިޔާސަތުން ރައްޔިތުންނަށް ފައިދާ ކުރާނެއެވެ.
incorrect: އާ ސިޔާސަތުން ޖެނެރަލް ޕަބްލިކަށް ފައިދާ ކުރާނެއެވެ.
error_description: ރައްޔިތުން is the established Dhivehi term for 'the general public' or 'the citizens/people'. The transliteration ޖެނެރަލް ޕަބްލިކ is not used in formal PO Dhivehi; it is an Anglicism that has no place in institutional text.

source: The minister arrived at the venue and addressed the gathering.
correct: ވަޒީރު ތަނަށް ވަޑައިގެން ހާޒިރިންނާ ބައްދަލުުކޮށް ވާހަކަ ދެއްކެވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ވަޒީރު ތަނަށް ވަޑައިގެންނެވިއެވެ. ދެން ހާޒިރިންނާ ބައްދަލުުކޮށް ވާހަކަ ދެއްކެވިއެވެ.
error_description: Dhivehi converb scaffold: sequential actions in a single sentence are joined using the converb form (-ގެން / -ކޮށް) rather than splitting into two finite clauses with a separate sentence. Using a full stop and a new sentence for each clause is a structural calque from English.
notes: Scaffold entry — needs native DV speaker review for morphological accuracy of converb forms. The core contrast (converb chain vs. finite-per-clause splitting) is linguistically attested.

source: The President signed the agreement and handed it to the delegation.
correct: ރައީސް އެއްބަސްވުުމުގައި ސޮއިކޮށް ވަފުދާ ހަވާލުުކޮށް ދެއްވިއެވެ.
incorrect: ރައީސް އެއްބަސްވުުމުގައި ސޮއިކުރެއްވިއެވެ. ދެން ވަފުދާ ހަވާލުުކޮށް ދެއްވިއެވެ.
error_description: Converb scaffold: the two actions (signing + handing over) form a single converb chain ending in one finite verb. Splitting into two separate sentences with two finite verbs is a structural English calque error.
notes: Scaffold entry — needs native DV speaker review. Converb chaining with -ކޮށް is the expected structure for consecutive actions by the same subject.

source: The official opened the file and read the contents aloud.
correct: ރަސްމީ ބޭފުޅާ ފައިލު ހުޅުުވައި ތެރޭގައި ހުރި ލިޔުުންތައް ބާރަށް ކިޔއިދެ ދިނެވެ.
incorrect: ރަސްމީ ބޭފުޅާ ފައިލު ހުޅުުވިއެވެ. ދެން ތެރޭގައި ހުރި ލިޔުުންތައް ބާރަށް ކިޔއި ދިނެވެ.
error_description: Converb scaffold: sequential actions (opening + reading) by the same subject should be chained with the converb form (-ވައި) into one clause ending with a single finite verb, not split into two finite-verb sentences.
notes: Scaffold entry — needs native DV speaker review and correction of both Dhivehi fields before use in eval. The converb form ހުޅުުވައި is plausible but the reading verb construction requires native verification.

source: The committee reviewed the report and submitted its recommendations.
correct: ކޮމިޓީ ރިޕޯޓު ދިރާސާކޮށް ހުށަހަޅަންޖެހޭ ލަފާތައް ހުށަހެޅިއެވެ.
incorrect: ކޮމިޓީ ރިޕޯޓު ދިރާސާކުރިއެވެ. ދެން ހުށަހަޅަންޖެހޭ ލަފާތައް ހުށަހެޅިއެވެ.
error_description: Converb scaffold: the two actions (reviewing + submitting) share the same subject (committee) and should be expressed as a converb chain ending in one finite verb. Two separate sentences with two finite verbs is the English-calque error pattern.
notes: Scaffold entry — needs native DV speaker review for morphological accuracy of the converb chain.

source: The delegation departed from Male' and arrived at the island in the afternoon.
correct: ވަފުުދު މާލެ އިން ފުރައިގެން ހަވީރު ރަށަށް ގެންދިޔައެވެ.
incorrect: ވަފުުދު މާލެ އިން ފުުރިއެވެ. ދެން ހަވީރު ރަށަށް ގެންދިޔައެވެ.
error_description: Converb scaffold: departure and arrival are sequential actions by the same subject that form a natural converb chain (-ގެން links the two clauses). Splitting into two separate finite-verb sentences is a structural calque from English that is unnatural in formal Dhivehi.
notes: Scaffold entry — needs native DV speaker review. The converb form ފުރައިގެން (from ފުުރި) is plausible but requires native verification.
